questions.prompt: Under Oregon jobsite safety law, which statement best reflects ORS chapter 654 duties?
choices: Only employees must comply with safety rules on a jobsite.
Employers must furnish a safe and healthful workplace and comply with applicable safety rules.
Contractors only need to follow federal OSHA text exactly as written in 29 CFR.
Safety obligations apply only after an accident has occurred.
questions.explanation: ORS chapter 654 includes a broad employer duty to provide a safe and healthful workplace and to comply with applicable safety rules and orders, making choice two the best answer.

questions.prompt: Which Oregon construction rule set requires employers to train workers exposed to fall hazards?
questions.explanation: Oregon fall protection and related worker training requirements for construction are covered in Division 3 Subdivision M, including OAR 437-003-0503 training duties.

questions.prompt: In an Oregon trench 4 feet deep, what is a required compliance element?
choices: No egress is required if workers have hard hats.
Workers may travel any distance laterally as long as shoring is present.
A safe means of egress must be provided so lateral travel does not exceed 25 feet.
Excavation inspections are only required after heavy rain.
questions.explanation: Subdivision P requires safe means of egress in trench excavations at 4 feet or more depth, with no more than 25 feet of lateral travel to exit.
